question: Dimercaptosuccinic acid (DMSA) scan or ultrasound in screening for vesicoureteral reflux among children with urinary tract infections (a diagnostic-test-accuracy review: in children with urinary tract infection, what is the accuracy of DMSA renal scan or ultrasound for detecting vesicoureteral reflux?)
source: CLEF TAR 2018 Task 2 (Testing), content-level qrels — finally-included studies of Cochrane review CD010657.

protocol.seeds: none — proceed under the no-seed workflow
protocol.framework: diagnostic (PIRD / diagnostic PICO). Index tests = DMSA renal scan OR renal/urinary ultrasound; target condition = vesicoureteral reflux; population = children with urinary tract infection.
protocol.essential_concepts: vesicoureteral reflux; index test (DMSA scan OR renal/urinary ultrasound). Decide essential block structure yourself per the skill's anti-pattern guidance (prefer fewer AND blocks).
protocol.optional_blocks: DECLINE all optional secondary AND blocks — do NOT add a reference-standard / diagnostic-accuracy block, and do NOT require a separate children/UTI population AND block (handle the paediatric/UTI population at screening to protect recall).
protocol.limits: none (no date, language, species, or age limits)
protocol.final_cleanup: remove exact duplicate terms and genuinely zero-hit phrases (after ruling out typos); keep all recall-bearing terms; apply automatically without asking.

notes: Diagnostic-test-accuracy review. Gold set = 35 included studies. No hand-built baseline strategy: use evals/generate.py (Phase 2) to have the skill build one. Several gold studies are from the 1990s; the harness reports reachable vs. unreachable separately.
